title: Traders Boost BOE and ECB Rate-Hike Bets After Oil Price Surge
summary: Traders boosted wagers on Bank of England and European Central Bank interest-rate hikes after surging oil prices reignited inflation fears.

title: China Widens Australian Canola Trade to Include Private Crushers
summary: China will allow its private oilseed crushers to buy Australian canola, as the two countries move to normalize a trade that’s largely been halted since the start of the decade.

title: Korean Won Climbs to Two-Month High on Hynix ADR Inflows
summary: The South Korean won advanced to its strongest level since May, supported by dollar inflows tied to SK Hynix Inc.’s American depositary receipts, according to people familiar with the matter.

title: Iran Sneaks Out Tankers Via Hormuz as Trump Amps Up Threats
summary: Iran has reverted to its well-used tactic of going dark to move oil tankers through the Strait of Hormuz in recent days, even before the US said it would reimpose a blockade of the country’s ports.

title: Rathbones Cut Gilt Exposure in Case Burnham ‘Does a Truss’
summary: Rathbones Asset Management has reduced its holdings of UK government bonds to avoid a potential selloff if Andy Burnham boosts spending and raises borrowing.

title: Africa’s $219 Billion Fund Suspends CEO as Internal Rift Deepens
summary: The board of Africa’s largest state-run fund suspended Chief Executive Officer Patrick Dlamini in a late-night move that highlighted the deepening feud at the $219 billion asset manager.

title: Oil Tops $86 a Barrel as Trump Reinstates Naval Blockade on Iran
summary: Brent oil rose above $86 a barrel for the first time in a month, as US President Donald Trump reimposed a blockade on Iranian ships transiting the Strait of Hormuz and tensions rose after attacks on two tankers.

title: US Stocks, Treasuries Steady Before Warsh and CPI: Markets Wrap
summary: US stocks steadied as traders refrained from making big bets ahead of a jam-packed day that will see the unofficial start of the earnings season, along with testimony from Fed Chair Kevin Warsh and US inflation data.

title: Klarna Plans New Buy-Now-Pay-Later SRT as It Targets US Growth
summary: Klarna is seeking to offload credit risk tied to a portfolio of ‘buy now, pay later loans’, in a deal aimed at freeing up capital as it looks to accelerate its international expansion plans.

title: SK Hynix Shares Rebound After Early Rout as AI Jitters Persist
summary: SK Hynix Inc. erased an early plunge to climb more than 3% in Seoul on Tuesday, underscoring the extreme volatility gripping one of the world’s biggest beneficiaries of the artificial intelligence boom.

title: India Price Risks Mount on Renewed US-Iran Tensions, El Nino
summary: India’s stronger-than-expected inflation reading for June has left policymakers facing a more uncertain outlook, with renewed tensions around the Strait of Hormuz threatening to push up oil prices and a developing El Niño raising the risk of higher food costs.

title: Senegal Bondholders Start Grouping as Nation Seeks Adviser
summary: Some creditors holding Senegal’s sovereign bonds have begun informal talks to potentially form a group if the government moves forward with a debt rework, people familiar with the matter said.

title: Trillion Dollar Chip Rout Trains Spotlight on TSMC and ASML Results
summary: At a time of acute investor unease over a selloff in AI-inflated tech stocks, this week’s results from Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co. and ASML Holding NV carry even greater significance than usual.

title: BP Flags $1 Billion Writedown From Low Carbon Business
summary: BP Plc said it expects to write down another $1 billion from energy transition assets in the second quarter, as the British major continues the painstaking work of re-orientating itself toward its core oil and gas business.

title: Europe Is Set for the Strongest Earnings Growth in Three Years
summary: European companies are set to post their strongest profit growth in three years in the second-quarter reporting season, buoyed by expected blowout results from oil majors, banks and artificial intelligence winners.

title: Warsh to Move Markets More Than He Wants: 3-Minutes MLIV
summary: Anna Edwards, Tom Mackenzie and Mark Cudmore break down today's key themes for analysts and investors on "Bloomberg: The Opening Trade."  (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Horizons Middle East & Africa 7/14/2026
summary: Horizons Middle East & Africa is your daily spotlight on one of the world's fastest-growing regions. Live from Dubai, we bring you the latest global markets and analysis, plus news-making interviews, with a special focus on MEA. All that and more, as you head to the office in the Gulf, pause for lunch in Hong Kong, or start your day in London or Johannesburg.  (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Middle East War Set to Push Back LNG Glut by a Year, BNEF Says
summary: The liquefied natural gas market will move into oversupply in 2028, a year later than previously forecast, as war in the Middle East and major project delays push back the emergence of a global glut, according to BloombergNEF.

title: Deutsche Bank and World Bank Team Up for Trade Finance Platform
summary: The World Bank and Deutsche Bank AG are starting a €1 billion trade finance platform for frontier and emerging markets.

title: Improving Hiring Trends Boost Embattled European Recruiters
summary: European staffing companies’ better-than-feared numbers and cautiously optimistic outlook are pointing to a stabilizing labor market after years of downturn.

title: US-Iran Clashes Lift Oil, Rate Hike Bets | Insight with Haslinda Amin 7/14/2026
summary: Insight with Haslinda Amin, a daily news program featuring in-depth, high-profile interviews and analysis to give viewers the complete picture on the stories that matter. The show features prominent leaders spanning the worlds of business, finance, politics and culture. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Bank of America Taps Pauli as Asia Pacific Capital Markets Chief
summary: Bank of America Corp. has promoted Thorsten Pauli to lead the bank’s Asia Pacific Global Capital Markets unit, adding to a flurry of changes in the region.

title: Indian Insurers Ask for Wider Use of Swaps to Hedge Rate Risks
summary: Some Indian insurers are asking regulators to allow them to use more interest-rate hedging tools as they prepare to play a larger role in the country’s money markets, according to people familiar with the matter.

title: Asian Oil Buyers Look to US Crude Again as Iran War Intensifies
summary: The escalation of hostilities between the Washington and Tehran and the virtual standstill in observable traffic through the Strait of Hormuz is prompting Asian oil refiners to try and buy more US crude.

title: Japan Bonds Jump After Katayama’s GPIF Remarks, Strong Auction
summary: Japanese government bonds climbed on Tuesday after remarks by Finance Minister Satsuki Katayama boosted sentiment toward sovereign debt, and as a 20-year auction attracted strong investor demand.

title: Ashmore Sees $1.3 Billion of Inflows as Clients Embrace EM Funds
summary: Ashmore Group Plc reported net inflows for the three months through June as the emerging-markets-focused asset manager’s funds performed well with markets roiled by the uncertainty sparked by the war in Iran.

title: China's Oil Imports Drop to Lowest in Nearly a Decade | The China Show | 7/14/2026
summary: “Bloomberg: The China Show” is your definitive source for news and analysis on the world's second-biggest economy. From politics and policy to tech and trends, Yvonne Man and David Ingles give global investors unique insight, delivering in-depth discussions with the newsmakers who matter. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Bond Yields Jump as Traders Add to Rate-Hike Bets
Trump Reinstates Iran Blockade, Plans Hormuz Ship Fee
summary: President Donald Trump reinstated the US blockade of Iranian ships transiting the Strait of Hormuz and demanded a 20% reimbursement for all other cargo shipped via the waterway. Bloomberg's Stuart Livingstone-Wallace and Marisks CEO discuss the implications. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Oil Advances Above $85 on Hormuz Tensions
summary: Brent oil rose above $85 a barrel for the first time in a month, as US President Donald Trump reimposed a blockade on Iranian ships transiting the Strait of Hormuz and demanded payment for all other cargo. Bloomberg's Rong Wei Neo reports. (Source: Bloomberg)
